Scenario.title: Juicy Mix Campaign: Healthcare Data Exfiltration
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple A sophisticated phishing campaign targeting healthcare organizations to exfiltrate sensitive patient data and research findings through automated command execution mechanisms.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Application Log, Command
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Application Log Content, Command Execution

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker deploys spear-phishing emails containing malicious attachments to healthcare staff, exploiting trust relationships to gain initial access to internal systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The malicious image file, disguised as a legitimate medical report, triggers macro-enabled code execution when opened, establishing a persistent backdoor on infected endpoints.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker leverages command-line tools to automate the exfiltration of encrypted patient records and clinical trial data to external servers, masking traffic through DNS tunneling techniques.

Scenario.summary: A multi-stage attack leveraging phishing, malicious document execution, and automated data exfiltration to compromise healthcare IT infrastructure and steal sensitive medical data.
